meta.title: José Fernando García Pérez — Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Data Analyst
meta.description: Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Data Analyst. 20+ years GIS, 6+ years data analytics. Power BI, Python, Mapbox, SQL. Open to remote (USD/EUR). Medellín, Colombia.
meta.keywords: Geospatial Data Engineer, Data Analyst, GIS, Power BI, Python, Mapbox, SQL, Medellín, remote

hero.status: Open to work · Remote (USD/EUR) · Hybrid Colombia
hero.pre: Senior Geospatial Data Engineer & Data Analyst
hero.skills: GIS · Power BI · Python · Mapbox · SQL
hero.sub: Civil engineer with 20+ years in geospatial intelligence and 6+ years as a data analyst. I design pipelines that hold up under regulatory audits and ship dashboards that survive board meetings.

capabilities.title: What I deliver
capabilities.sub: Three roles, one engineer. Hire me for any of the following.

items.title: Cloud data pipelines & geospatial ETL
items.desc: Production pipelines from raw spatial data to clean analytics layers. Python automation, PostGIS, ArcPy, Azure Data Factory. Built for institutional scale.

items.title: Executive dashboards & semantic models
items.desc: Power BI dashboards with complex DAX, star and bridge models, Power Query M transformations. The kind that survives board scrutiny and audit cycles.

items.title: Spatial systems & national-standard databases
items.desc: Geodatabases under ANLA / IGAC standards. Multi-criteria spatial models. Hydrological networks. MAGNA-SIRGAS CTM12 reprojection. ArcGIS Pro, QGIS, PostGIS.

featured.desc: A retrieval-augmented generation system deployed in production for the City of Medellín. Lets urban-planning teams query the territorial plan in natural language and retrieve grounded answers with citations to the source document.
featured.disclaimer: Live system in production for the City of Medellín. Access credentials available on request for serious inquiries.
featured.userQuestion: ¿Cuáles son las áreas de protección ambiental establecidas en el POT?
featured.aiAnswerIntro: Según el POT vigente, las áreas de protección ambiental se clasifican en:
aiAnswerItems: 1. Áreas de retiro a corrientes hídricas
featured.previewNote: Sanitized UI preview · Actual interface includes authentication

amva.title: AMVA — Environmental compliance analytics
amva.summary: Four production Power BI dashboards for metropolitan permitting, sanctions, action plan, and urban tree-risk under citizen-transparency requirements.
amva.challenge: AMVA needed traceable compliance views across permit lifecycles, sanction flows, and tree-risk cases — with business-day logic, spatial context, and audit-ready KPIs.
amva.approach: Star and detail models in Power BI with complex DAX for deadlines and status transitions. Python/ArcPy pipelines maintain geodatabases and MAGNA-SIRGAS CTM12 layers feeding semantic models.
amva.outcome: Leadership and technical teams share one source of truth for permit status, sanctions, and risk monitoring — reducing manual reporting and improving response times on high-priority cases.

medellin-dap.title: Medellín DAP — Urban planning intelligence
medellin-dap.summary: Seven Power BI dashboards plus a subdirectorate web portfolio supporting POT revision, legal tracking, and participatory planning processes.
medellin-dap.challenge: DAP required coordinated analytics across juridical follow-up, strategic revision themes, participatory processes, and executive KPIs — while integrating spatial planning workflows.
medellin-dap.approach: Power Query M models with star/bridge patterns, executive dashboards for each workstream, and ArcPy for multicriteria spatial analysis. RAG assistant documented separately.
medellin-dap.outcome: Planning teams monitor POT revision milestones, legal commitments, and participation metrics from unified dashboards used in internal and transparency contexts.

rag-pot-medellin.title: RAG assistant — POT Medellín (322 pages)
rag-pot-medellin.summary: Production retrieval-augmented generation system letting planners query the territorial plan in Spanish with cited sources.
rag-pot-medellin.challenge: A 322-page legal/technical POT is impractical to search manually during meetings and revision cycles. Teams needed grounded answers, not hallucinations.
rag-pot-medellin.approach: Document chunking, vector retrieval, Spanish LLM orchestration, and citation-first response formatting. Deployed on Render with authenticated access for DAP teams.
rag-pot-medellin.outcome: ~5s average responses with page-level citations. Reduces time to locate norms on environmental protection, land use, and strategic revision topics.

demos.title: Owned demos & open data
demos.sub: Showpieces built on Colombian public datasets and open-source stacks — fully under my control for international portfolios.
demos.openDataDesc: Dashboard prototype using datos.gov.co and Medellín open-data catalogs — environmental and territorial indicators with reproducible Python/Power BI pipelines.
demos.openDataSoonDev: Demo URL configured in .env.local — add NEXT_PUBLIC_OPEN_DATA_DEMO_URL when published.
demos.ragDesc: Production RAG for Medellín POT (featured above). Next: open-source replica on a public regulation with GitHub repo.

about.title: About
about.name: José Fernando García Pérez
about.p1: Civil engineer (Universidad Nacional de Colombia, 2006) with three postgraduate specializations. Licensed under T.P. 05202139705 ANT.
about.p2: I started with maps. AutoCAD-to-ArcGIS migrations, PostgreSQL spatial databases, geodatabases for governments. Then the data scaled, the dashboards came, and DAX became part of the daily work. Today I build both layers — the spatial pipeline and the analytics on top.
about.p3: Currently expanding into Azure, Databricks and Microsoft Fabric. Working remote, hybrid, or on-site for clients worldwide.
about.cvNote: Place your PDF at public/cv-jose-fernando-garcia.pdf to enable download.
